description: Standard single-city plan using cached Rome data. Baseline for speed and quality.
input: 5-day Rome trip, couple, $200/day, ancient history focus, must see Vatican and Colosseum, starting 2026-06-10

checks.comment: Date is within 16 days of eval run — should get real forecast badges.

description: Mobility constraint — cane user, max 1.5km/day walking. Solver must note accessibility.
input: 5-day Rome, couple, $180/day, one partner uses a cane — max 1.5km walking per day, loves food and art

checks.comment: At least one of these words should appear in the itinerary notes.

description: Two-city Portugal trip. Tests multi-city flow, transport day, per-city hotel.
input: 7-day Portugal trip, couple, $150/day, food and culture, starting 2026-09-01

checks.comment: Must show city banners and a travel day with transport emoji (🚆/🚌).

description: Three-city Japan trip. Tests 3-leg multi-city, Shinkansen transport, no restaurant repeats across cities.
input: 10-day Japan trip, couple, $250/day, temples and food, must see Fushimi Inari shrine, starting 2026-10-15

checks.comment: Fushimi Inari is in Kyoto — must appear there. Two transport days expected.

description: Budget traveler, cached Bangkok data. Tests budget constraint enforcement.
input: 4-day Bangkok trip, solo backpacker, $80/day, street food and temples, no luxury restaurants

checks.comment: No high-end restaurants should appear for an $80/day budget.

description: Short 3-day trip, cached Florence data. Tests quality on minimal duration.
input: 3-day Florence trip, couple, $200/day, Renaissance art focus, must see Uffizi Gallery

description: Query with no start_date — orchestrator must skip weather silently and still produce full itinerary.

checks.comment: No date → no weather call → no badges. Itinerary must still be complete.

description: Vegetarian dietary restriction must propagate to restaurant selection.
input: 4-day Rome trip, couple, $150/day, vegetarian diet, loves art and history

checks.comment: The word vegetarian must appear somewhere in the itinerary (dietary note per slot or header).
